## [00:00 - 01:33]

This is a Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer and today we're going to talk about why your ad budget isn't spending. Let's go. Why isn't your Facebook ads budget spending? There are four potential reasons. First, your audience is too small. Second, you're using campaign budget optimization along with ad set spending limits. Check to see what those limits are and maybe don't use them. Third, you set a cost per result or a big control within your optimization for delivery. Sure you can do this but if it's too aggressive it will impact your delivery. And fourth, you're optimizing for an action that isn't leading to results. This is the algorithms way of protecting you. Trying to prevent you from wasting money. It's basically Facebook saying hey dude this isn't working. Maybe you shouldn't be spending this money. Beyond that make sure to check the delivery column within your ads view. If there are any specific issues with delivery you will see a warning or alert of some kind there. The bottom line is that delivery and budget spending issues are largely caused by an attempt to restrict the algorithm. Hope that helps. Follow for more. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
